The '''I.M. mine''' was a simple type of land mine that could be set to explode in two functions: either by timeset or by proximity. It was manufactured by [[Imperial Machines]].<ref name="LucasArtsCite-The Force is With You in New Star Wars Epic, ''Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II'', the Highly Anticipated ''Dark Forces'' Multiplayer Sequel From LucasArts">{{LucasArts|url=pages/Announcement.537.html|text=The Force is With You in New Star Wars Epic, ''Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II'', the Highly Anticipated ''Dark Forces'' Multiplayer Sequel From LucasArts}}</ref>

The mines were used by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Stacks of such mines were found in several installations such as the [[Imperial Detention Center]] on [[Orinackra]] where [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] agent [[Kyle Katarn]] came upon these weapons.<ref name="Star Wars: Dark Forces">''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]''</ref>

{{Weapon-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
I.M. mines first appeared in a secret area in the [[Wikipedia:game demo|demo]] version of ''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]'', where they were referred to as "[[Wikipedia:claymore mine|claymore mines]]"; in the finished game, the I.M. mines appear first in the Orinackra level.

In the game, the mines also can be used in order to propel Kyle [[Wikipedia:Rocket_jumping|while jumping]] (with some unavoidable damage), and thus reach some hard-to-access areas, especially in order to collect bonuses.<ref name="Dark Forces Official Player's Guide">''[[Dark Forces Official Player's Guide]]''</ref>

According to the ''[[Dark Forces Official Player's Guide]]'', "Claymore" was the nickname of the weapon while designing the game, without making clear if it was also called like that in-universe. The [[M1C-F Claymore Mine]] appeared later in ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''.
